rationale: Both correctly validate the core loader changes (direct loaders apply the mapper, delegated loaders correctly rely on the outer AutoWeightsLoader) and both independently catch the latent gap — hunyuan_image3_transformer.py still calling the removed get_cache_scale API — with concrete file/line evidence. Y grounds its validation in actual upstream vLLM source (AutoWeightsLoader, weight_utils.py, fp8.py), echoing the human reviewer's own rationale almost verbatim, and its three findings are tighter and less redundant than X's five overlapping test-coverage nitpicks (fake mapper only handling k_proj, no real quant config, test-file-removal ambiguity) that dilute precision. Y's APPROVE-with-non-blocking-notes verdict also matches the actual outcome (LGTM/APPROVED) whereas X's 'FOLLOW-UP REQUIRED' overstates severity relative to ground truth.
